title: Using Free Online PDF Tools
description: Learn to use free online tools like ilovepdf.com and smallpdf.com for PDF conversion, editing, and management.
objectives: Convert PDF to Word and back
Use OCR to make scanned PDFs editable
content:
When You Don't Have Adobe Acrobat

Not every workplace has expensive PDF software. Fortunately, many free online tools can handle common PDF tasks. Two reliable and popular options are ilovepdf.com and smallpdf.com. These tools work in your web browser and do not require installation.

Common PDF Tasks

TaskTool ExampleWhen to Use
PDF to Wordilovepdf.com PDF to WordWhen you need to edit a scanned or locked PDF
Word to PDFsmallpdf.com Word to PDFWhen you need a professional final copy
Merge PDFsilovepdf.com MergeWhen combining multiple documents for submission
Split PDFssmallpdf.com SplitWhen a file is too large or needs separate sections
Compress PDFsilovepdf.com CompressWhen emailing a file over the size limit
OCR (Scan to Text)smallpdf.com OCRWhen you need to edit a scanned image PDF

Workplace Scenario

A client emails you a scanned contract as a PDF. You need to change one paragraph. You do not have Adobe Acrobat. You go to ilovepdf.com, use the 'PDF to Word' tool, upload the file, download the editable Word document, make your changes, and then use 'Word to PDF' to convert it back. You now have a clean, updated PDF ready to send back to the client.

Security Warning

Free online tools are convenient, but be cautious with confidential documents. If the document contains sensitive information (salaries, contracts, personal data), use offline software or a paid service with privacy guarantees. For routine documents like public reports or templates, free tools are perfectly safe.

downloads.title: PDF Tools Guide
downloads.description: How to use ilovepdf.com and smallpdf.com

exercise: Go to ilovepdf.com and explore the following tools: PDF to Word, Word to PDF, Merge PDF, and Compress PDF. Create a short Word document, convert it to PDF, then convert the PDF back to Word. Note how well the formatting is preserved. Merge two PDFs into one. Compress a PDF and compare the file size before and after. Write a one-paragraph summary of which tool you found most useful and why.

quiz.question: Which tool would you use to edit a scanned PDF that you cannot edit directly?
quiz.explanation: A PDF to Word converter creates an editable document from a scanned or locked PDF, allowing you to make changes.

quiz.question: When should you compress a PDF?
options: When you want to delete it
When the file is too large to email or upload
When you want to add more pages
When you want to change the font
quiz.explanation: Compression reduces file size, making it easier to share via email or upload to websites with size limits.

quiz.question: Why should you be careful with confidential documents on free online tools?
options: Privacy and security of sensitive data cannot be guaranteed
quiz.explanation: Free online tools upload your file to external servers. For sensitive documents, use offline software or trusted paid services with privacy guarantees.

quiz.question: Which ilovepdf.com tool would you use to combine a cover letter and a CV into one file?
quiz.explanation: The Merge PDF tool combines multiple PDFs into a single document, which is ideal for combining a cover letter and CV.
